apps.name: Pairadox
apps.summary: Mix two ingredients into a creative challenge or a funny story with a three-panel symbolic storyboard.
apps.description: A tactile idea game with challenge and story modes. Drag, tap, or use the keyboard to load two ingredients, then create a prompt or a complete local-only comedy with animated symbolic scenes and optional sound.

config.instructions: Drag or tap two ingredients into the forge. Choose Challenge for a prompt or Story for a generated comedy with symbolic scenes, then remix whenever you want another result.
outcomes: Invent a game that uses both ingredients and explain it in one sentence.
Create a thirty-second story in which both ingredients solve the same problem.
Design a fictional product inspired by both ingredients and give it a slogan.
Make a harmless challenge for a friend that includes both ingredients.
Describe a new holiday built around both ingredients.
Turn both ingredients into the rules of a miniature competition.
Imagine both ingredients are clues. Explain the mystery they reveal.
Combine both ingredients into an unusual but believable superpower.
Design a one-button arcade game where both ingredients change the rules.
Create a touch interaction where dragging one ingredient transforms the other.
Invent a sound cue for each ingredient, then combine them into a success jingle.
Turn the pair into a twenty-second physical challenge with three escalating rounds.
Sketch a moving icon that transitions from the first ingredient into the second.
Make a cooperative mini-game playable with one finger from each player.
Design a harmless boss battle where one ingredient is the player and the other is the obstacle.
Add a replay twist that changes the challenge after every imaginary win.

apps.name: Time Sense
apps.summary: Choose a quick or deep session and test how accurately you feel time passing.
apps.description: An arcade-like perception game with three session lengths, a moving canvas spark, hidden timing, optional local sound, scoring, touch, pointer, keyboard support, and clean resource teardown.

config.instructions: Choose Quick, Classic, or Endurance, then start the hidden timer and stop when the target duration feels complete.

apps.name: Fair Choice
apps.summary: Spin an animated fairness wheel that gently reduces repeat winners.
apps.description: A responsive canvas wheel for chores, turns, restaurants, or group choices. It supports touch, pointer, keyboard, optional local audio, recent-winner balancing, duplicate protection, and clean cancellation when closed.

config.instructions: Add two to twelve choices, then tap the animated wheel or use the keyboard. Recent winners become temporarily less likely to repeat.

apps.name: Tiny Step
apps.summary: Build a moving three-step trail matched to your goal and current energy.
apps.description: A local-only momentum tool with an animated SVG trail, draggable step selector, touch and keyboard controls, optional audio, flexible completion tracking, and energy-aware actions.

config.instructions: Name the goal, choose your real energy level, then build a three-step trail. Drag the marker, tap a step, or use the keyboard to inspect and complete the route.

apps.name: Constraint Spark
apps.summary: Draw freely or race through three timed creative constraints with one strategic swap.
apps.description: A tactile 16-card constraint deck with Explore and Three-card Sprint modes, 30/60/90-second pacing, scoring, one strategic swap, animated SVG motion, touch, pointer, keyboard controls, optional local audio, and no-repeat reshuffling.

config.instructions: Explore the animated deck freely, or choose a 30, 60, or 90-second three-card sprint. Complete each round for points and use one strategic swap when a constraint does not fit.
items: Make the ending explain the beginning.
Remove the feature you would normally show first.
Use repetition as the organizing principle.
Design for someone using only one hand.
Let one deliberate imperfection remain visible.
Make the smallest useful version possible.
Replace one instruction with a discoverable interaction.
Make one element respond differently to a tap, a drag, and a keyboard input.
Build a loop that becomes harder every thirty seconds.
Use motion to reveal state instead of adding explanatory text.
Let the player discover one rule by causing a harmless failure.
Design the interaction around a single moving object.
Add an optional sound cue that communicates success without words.
Make the experience fully playable with one finger and the arrow keys.
Turn the final state into the starting condition for the next round.
